title: Either Or
description: Learn to handle both true and false cases with if/else statements.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Sometimes you want to do something when the condition is true, and something ELSE when it's false.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: That's where the 'else' statement comes in!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So it's like: if this, do that, otherwise do something else?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Let's check if our crops are ready. First, type: crop_ready = False

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Our crops are not ready yet!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Now let's write an if statement. Type: if crop_ready:

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Notice we can use a boolean directly! No need for == True.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Add the action for when crops are ready (indented). Type:     print("Time to harvest!")

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Now add the else. It should be at the same level as 'if' (not indented). Type: else:

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Perfect! Now we need to add what happens when it's false.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Add the action for when crops are NOT ready (indented). Type:     print("Still growing...")

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Now your code handles both cases!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Click play! Since crop_ready is False, you should see 'Still growing...'

objectives.description: Click play to run your code

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: It worked! Your code chose the else path because crop_ready was False!

postDialogues.name: Qubit
postDialogues.content: And if I change crop_ready to True, it would show 'Time to harvest!'?

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Exactly! Now you can handle any situation with if/else!
